mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate expanding aggregate wage income and consumption capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through higher expected corporate revenues.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2013 payrolls at 136038K showing sustained growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS change and 20-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, equities reprice on forward guidance expectations because conventional policy space is exhausted; this environment produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days as duration risk is shifted to the Fed balance sheet.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2013 effective fed funds rate at 0.09%
failure_condition: In months with FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, 60-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 60% of instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and a decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirm a demand-labor virtuous cycle where rising incomes feed back into consumer spending; this joint condition generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days that neither variable produces independently.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2013 retail sales at 424481M and unemployment at 7.4%
failure_condition: The interaction term in a bivariate regression does not improve adjusted R-squared by more than 0.02, or positive return hit rate is below 55% when both conditions hold.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive 5-day S&P 500 returns specifically when the economy is operating below potential, as measured by high unemployment; in this regime, output gains signal margin recovery rather than overheating.
failure_condition: In high-unemployment months, INDPRO acceleration fails to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A three-month annualized CPI inflation rate above 2.5% signals tighter future monetary policy and erodes real corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2013 CPI at 233.3 with moderate inflation
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or Rank IC is greater than -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 800 thousand units signal durable construction demand and upstream commodity and labor demand; when this threshold is crossed, it predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days via cyclical earnings expansion.
failure_condition: When HOUST is above 800, 60-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of months over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of falling unemployment (UNRATE) and low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a Goldilocks scenario where labor market tightening does not yet threaten margins because financing costs are negligible, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2013 UNRATE at 7.4% and FEDFUNDS at 0.09%
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of months, or the interaction term coefficient is not significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) captures the broadest measure of domestic output expansion and corporate revenue growth, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2013 real GDP at 15648.7B chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
